notes: Copr chroots the release build targets (publish-copr submits '.targets | keys'). epel-9/epel-10 cover AlmaLinux/Rocky/RHEL 9/10.

The engine plugin is ABI-bound to the server's minor version, so the spec builds against the vendored MySQL source headers whose series matches the chroot's native MySQL: EL9's default module is 8.0; Fedora and EL10 ship 8.4. The spec picks the series from %{?rhel} (see rpm/pinba-engine.spec).

On EL the MySQL client dev package (mysql-devel) and mysql-server come from the AppStream mysql module stream (mysql:8.0 on EL9); enable the mysql module for the epel chroots in the Copr project's Buildroot customization if the default stream does not resolve mysql-devel in mock.

MySQL flavor only for now. A pinba-engine-mariadb subpackage follows once MariaDB server headers are vendored the same way as vendor/mysql-headers-8.x (they are generated by scripts/prepare-mariadb-headers.sh; a minimal vendored subset is the pending step).
